_meta.notes: Seed dataset. Edges are pedagogical (must-understand-before), not historical (influenced-by). Each node needs expert review before production use. Key concepts and readings are illustrative — the syllabus generator will eventually replace static recommendations.

nodes.label: Critical Thinking
nodes.description: Argument analysis, fallacy identification, reasoning evaluation

nodes.label: Formal Logic
nodes.description: Propositional and predicate logic, validity, soundness

nodes.label: Ancient Greek Philosophy
nodes.description: Pre-Socratics, Socrates, the birth of Western philosophy

nodes.label: Plato
nodes.description: Theory of Forms, the Republic, epistemology, political philosophy

nodes.label: Aristotle
nodes.description: Logic, metaphysics, ethics, natural philosophy, politics

nodes.label: Epistemology (Intro)
nodes.description: Knowledge, belief, justification, truth, skepticism

nodes.label: Rationalism
nodes.description: Descartes, Spinoza, Leibniz — reason as primary source of knowledge

nodes.label: Empiricism
nodes.description: Locke, Berkeley, Hume — experience as primary source of knowledge

nodes.label: Kantian Epistemology
nodes.description: Synthetic a priori, categories of understanding, transcendental idealism

nodes.label: Pragmatism
nodes.description: Peirce, James, Dewey — truth as what works in practice

nodes.label: Contemporary Epistemology
nodes.description: Gettier problems, reliabilism, virtue epistemology, social epistemology

nodes.label: Metaphysics (Intro)
nodes.description: Being, existence, substance, causation, identity, time

nodes.label: Mind-Body Dualism
nodes.description: Descartes's dualism, interaction problem, property dualism

nodes.label: Materialism / Physicalism
nodes.description: Everything is physical; eliminativism, reductionism

nodes.label: Idealism
nodes.description: Berkeley, Hegel — reality is fundamentally mental or conceptual

nodes.label: Modal Logic & Possible Worlds
nodes.description: Necessity, possibility, Kripke semantics, essence

nodes.label: Free Will & Determinism
nodes.description: Compatibilism, libertarianism, hard determinism, moral responsibility

nodes.label: Ethics (Intro)
nodes.description: Moral reasoning, the good, right action, value theory

nodes.label: Virtue Ethics
nodes.description: Aristotle, MacIntyre — character, flourishing, the virtues

nodes.label: Deontology
nodes.description: Kant's moral philosophy, categorical imperative, duty

nodes.label: Utilitarianism
nodes.description: Bentham, Mill — maximizing happiness, consequentialism

nodes.label: Metaethics
nodes.description: Moral realism, expressivism, error theory, constructivism

nodes.label: Applied Ethics
nodes.description: Bioethics, environmental ethics, AI ethics, business ethics

nodes.label: Political Philosophy (Intro)
nodes.description: Justice, authority, rights, the state, social contract

nodes.label: Social Contract Theory
nodes.description: Hobbes, Locke, Rousseau — legitimacy of political authority

nodes.label: Liberalism
nodes.description: Mill, Rawls — individual liberty, justice as fairness

nodes.label: Marxism
nodes.description: Historical materialism, class struggle, alienation, ideology

nodes.label: Feminist Philosophy
nodes.description: de Beauvoir, Butler — gender, power, knowledge, justice

nodes.label: Postcolonial Philosophy
nodes.description: Fanon, Said, Spivak — power, identity, epistemic injustice

nodes.label: Philosophy of Mind
nodes.description: Consciousness, intentionality, qualia, the hard problem

nodes.label: Functionalism
nodes.description: Mental states as functional roles — Putnam, Fodor

nodes.label: Consciousness Studies
nodes.description: Hard problem, neural correlates, panpsychism, IIT

nodes.label: Philosophy of Language
nodes.description: Meaning, reference, truth, speech acts — Frege, Russell, Wittgenstein

nodes.label: Wittgenstein
nodes.description: Tractatus, Philosophical Investigations, language games, forms of life

nodes.label: Hermeneutics
nodes.description: Interpretation theory — Gadamer, Ricoeur, the hermeneutic circle

nodes.label: Philosophy of Science
nodes.description: Demarcation, explanation, theory change — Popper, Kuhn, Lakatos

nodes.label: Kuhn & Scientific Revolutions
nodes.description: Paradigms, normal science, incommensurability

nodes.label: Philosophy of Physics
nodes.description: Space, time, quantum mechanics interpretation, laws of nature

nodes.label: Phenomenology
nodes.description: Husserl, intentionality, epoché, structures of experience

nodes.label: Existentialism
nodes.description: Heidegger, Sartre, Camus — existence, authenticity, absurdity, freedom

nodes.label: Structuralism
nodes.description: Saussure, Lévi-Strauss — underlying structures of culture and meaning

nodes.label: Poststructuralism
nodes.description: Derrida, Foucault, Deleuze — deconstruction, power, difference

nodes.label: Critical Theory
nodes.description: Frankfurt School — Adorno, Habermas, reason, ideology, emancipation

nodes.label: Aesthetics
nodes.description: Beauty, taste, art, the sublime — Kant, Hegel, Danto

nodes.label: Buddhist Philosophy
nodes.description: Dependent origination, emptiness, Nāgārjuna, consciousness

nodes.label: Confucianism
nodes.description: Ren, li, junzi — virtue, ritual, social harmony

nodes.label: Daoism
nodes.description: Dao, wu-wei, Laozi, Zhuangzi — naturalness, spontaneity

nodes.label: Comparative Philosophy
nodes.description: Cross-tradition dialogue, translation problems, universal vs relative
